subject: Public Health
title: Typhoid Fever
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Typhoid Fever.

hook.text: Typhoid fever infects 11 million people yearly—spread through water contaminated with human feces.

buildup.text: Salmonella typhi causes prolonged fever, headache, and abdominal pain—untreated, it kills 1 in 5.

discovery.text: Mary Mallon—'Typhoid Mary'—was the first known healthy carrier, spreading typhoid while showing no symptoms.

twist.text: Drug-resistant typhoid is spreading—a new conjugate vaccine offers hope where antibiotics are failing.

climax.text: Like cholera, typhoid disappears when communities gain access to clean water and safe sanitation.

punchline.text: Typhoid thrives where sewage meets drinking water.

quiz.question: Who was the first known healthy typhoid carrier?
